description:
A small CSV extract has been placed in the directory  
    “/home/user/data”.  
    1. Examine the file “/home/user/data/sales.csv”.  
       • It is comma-separated and its **first line is a header**.  
    2. Using a single terminal command of your choice (a pipeline is
       acceptable but must be entered as **one** command line) filter the
       file so that **only the rows whose “email” field ends with the
       domain “.edu” remain** (the header must stay at the top).  
       • Treat matching as case-insensitive.  
       • Use a regular-expression based tool (e.g. grep, awk, sed, perl,
         etc.) rather than a dedicated CSV processor.  
    3. Save the filtered result to “/home/user/output/edu_customers.csv”.  
    4. Create a short processing log at
       “/home/user/output/filter.log” with **exactly two lines** in the
       following format (the numbers shown here are placeholders; replace
       them with the real values you observe):  
       
           TOTAL_ROWS=<number_of_data_rows_in_source_file>
           EDU_ROWS=<number_of_matching_rows_written>
       
       • TOTAL_ROWS counts only data rows (exclude the header).  
       • EDU_ROWS must equal the number of data rows present in
         “edu_customers.csv”.  
       
    Verification checks:
       • The file “/home/user/output/edu_customers.csv” must exist and
         contain the original header plus only “.edu” rows (case-insensitive
         match on “.edu” at the end of the email field).  
       • “/home/user/output/filter.log” must exist, contain exactly two
         lines, be newline-terminated, and follow the key=value format
         shown above with correct integer counts.  
       • No extra files or lines should be produced inside
         “/home/user/output”.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:
    1. /home/user/data/sales.csv  
       Contents (11 lines total, 1 header + 10 data rows):
       
           order_id,customer,email,amount
           1001,Alice Smith,alice.smith@example.com,39.95
           1002,Bob Jones,bjones@STATE.EDU,15.00
           1003,Carla Ruiz,carla@college.edu,22.50
           1004,David Zhang,dzhang@corp.com,85.00
           1005,Eva Adams,evadams@school.edu,13.20
           1006,Fred Grant,grantf@business.org,44.10
           1007,Gina Hall,gina.hall@university.EDU,19.99
           1008,Henry Ito,henry@random.net,33.33
           1009,Ivy Keller,ivy.keller@tech.com,72.00
           1010,Jack Lee,jlee@liberalarts.edu,18.75
           
       • There are 10 data rows.
       • Rows whose email ends with “.edu” (case-insensitive) are
         lines 1002, 1003, 1005, 1007, and 1010 → 5 matches.
       
    Expected artefacts after successful completion:
    1. /home/user/output/edu_customers.csv  
       Exact contents (6 lines: header + 5 data rows):
       
           order_id,customer,email,amount
           1002,Bob Jones,bjones@STATE.EDU,15.00
           1003,Carla Ruiz,carla@college.edu,22.50
           1005,Eva Adams,evadams@school.edu,13.20
           1007,Gina Hall,gina.hall@university.EDU,19.99
           1010,Jack Lee,jlee@liberalarts.edu,18.75
       
       Must end with a trailing newline.
       
    2. /home/user/output/filter.log  
       Exact contents (two newline-terminated lines):
       
           TOTAL_ROWS=10
           EDU_ROWS=5
       
    The agent has write permission in /home/user/output (the directory
    already exists and is empty when the task begins).
